business.headline: Sensex opens 250 points higher, Nifty above 24,300; HDFC Bank up 2%The BSE Sensex was up 0.33% at 78,020.50, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 0.33% to trade at 24,349.95 around 9:30 am.

Sitting on idle gold? Government may soon give you a reason to earn from itThe Centre may soon unveil a revamped Gold Monetisation Scheme that allows jewellers to collect household gold deposits. The proposed shift is aimed at drawing idle gold into the formal system and reducing reliance on imports.

Want to become a crorepati through SIP? Here's how much to invest and for how longBecoming a crorepati is no longer just a dream for the wealthy. With the right SIP strategy and enough time, you could get there too, but how much do you really need to invest each month?

The company powering Nvidia's AI chips wants a $28 billion Wall Street debutSK Hynix is set to launch a $28 billion US listing after a sharp rally driven by AI chip demand. The offering underlines how companies supplying the hardware behind artificial intelligence are drawing huge investor interest.

Maersk places fresh order for 1,000 India-made shipping containersThe announcement came as Union Minister of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, Sarbananda Sonowal, unveiled the first export-import (EXIM) shipping container manufactured in India for the company at the Maersk-CONCOR Inland Container Depot in UP's Dadri.

E20 even powers F1 cars: Auto experts dismiss concerns, back govt amid backlashIndustry experts defended the rollout of E20 fuel, saying extensive testing found no concerns in compatible vehicles. They said the ethanol push is tied to lower emissions, defined standards and reduced oil dependence.

Carlsberg eyes India stock market listing; confidential IPO filing submittedCarlsberg said it has confidentially filed for an IPO of its Indian business. The proposed listing would allow a partial stake sale, subject to approvals and market conditions.

ITR 2026: Do you need to file two income tax returns? Tax department explainsThe new Income-tax Act has changed the way taxpayers talk about return filing, replacing "Assessment Year" with "Tax Year". But does that mean you'll have to file two ITRs? Here's what the tax department says.

Sensex, Nifty end higher on IT rally; HCLTech surges nearly 6%The BSE Sensex rose 261.79 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 77,763.91, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 95.15 points, or 0.39%, to close at 24,270.85.

IRDAI is finally going after the real reason behind insurance mis-sellingIRDAI is discussing a shift from upfront commissions to staggered payouts with the insurance industry. The proposed changes target mis-selling incentives and could tighten disclosure across distributors.

New EPFO Scheme: Will your employer contribute only Rs 1,800 to PF?The new EPFO Scheme, 2026 has raised an important question among salaried employees: will employers now limit their PF contribution to just Rs 1,800 a month? Here's what the new rules actually say.

Over Rs 9,300 crore lies unclaimed in 31 lakh EPF accounts, enough to build 3 IITsAn RTI response revealed that Rs 9,330 crore remains unclaimed in 30.91 lakh inoperative EPF accounts across India. The disclosure comes as the new EPF Scheme, 2026 takes effect, while details on Aadhaar-linked and high-value accounts remain undisclosed.

Planning to buy a plot in Noida? Read this Noida Authority warning firstThe Noida Authority has warned people against buying or selling land under specified khasra numbers in three villages. It said the parcels are acquired or notified land where illegal construction could bring financial and legal trouble.

Missed your EMI by a day? Here's how it can affect your credit scoreDid you miss your EMI by a day and instantly worry about your CIBIL score? Before you panic, here's what actually happens behind the scenes.

AI mega deal: Why HCLTech shares jump nearly 6% today?At around 11 am, HCLTech was trading nearly 6% higher. The rally also helped lift the broader Nifty IT index for the second straight session, even as AI-heavy markets in South Korea and Taiwan witnessed a sharp selloff.

Air India cuts fuel surcharge on North America, Europe flights as oil easesAir India has reduced temporary fuel surcharges on select long-haul routes from July 1. The move follows easing oil prices after the West Asia conflict had pushed up operating costs.
Sensex opens 400 points higher, Nifty above 24,300; HCLTech up 4%The BSE Sensex rose 507 points to 78,009.37, while the NSE Nifty50 gained nearly 170 points to trade at 24,346.25 in early trading.

Is the EPFO portal working today? Here's what PF subscribers should knowEPFO has said member and employer services are expected to resume from July 3 after another maintenance extension. The prolonged upgrade has delayed PF claims, passbook access and compliance tasks for nearly a week.

Zero tax in Form 16? You may still have to file an ITR. Here's whyEven if your employer has deducted no income tax because you qualify for the rebate under Section 87A, you may still be legally required to file an ITR in several situations.

EPF Scheme 2026: What employees and employers need to knowWith around eight crore active EPFO subscribers, the government hopes the updated scheme will make provident fund management simpler, more efficient and better suited to a digital-first workplace.

entertainment.headline: Why is Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj banned in India? Zee5 has removed Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj from its platform in India just days after releasing the film in its uncut version. Here's what happened

Satluj row: Director Honey Trehan shares message as ZEE5 removes Diljit Dosanjh film Director Honey Trehan posted a message of acceptance after ZEE5 removed Satluj in India within two days of release. The move has again thrown the long-delayed Diljit Dosanjh film into uncertainty.

No tension, everyone has downloaded: When Diljit predicted Satluj's OTT removal Days before Satluj was removed from ZEE5 in India, actor Diljit Dosanjh appeared to predict the film's fate on the streaming platform. During an Instagram Live session, he said he was not worried about the possibility and had "no tension" over it.

Singer Suchitra Krishnamoorthi reacts to X user defending Siya Goyal in Pune murder Actor-singer Suchitra Krishnamoorthi criticised an X user defending accused Siya Goyal in the Ketan Agarwal murder case, saying forced marriage cannot justify alleged criminal behaviour. She said there were legal options available and no culture can excuse violence.

Satluj removed from OTT: Internet rallies behind Diljit Dosanjh's film After ZEE5 removed Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj from its platform in India two days after release, the move triggered online outrage, with many backing the film.

Alpha box office Day 3: Alia Bhatt, Sharvari's spy drama wraps strong weekend Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's Alpha, which opened to decent numbers, posed a Sunday jump. The film's total India nett is now Rs 34 crore in three days.

Ssunita Ahuja refuses to eat on Lock Upp 2, wants to quit the show Ssunita Ahuja protested the food on Lock Upp and refused to eat. The row deepened as she broke down and recalled husband-actor Govinda's warning before entering the show.

I challenge the darkness: Diljit Dosanjh on Satluj being taken off ZEE5 Actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh broke his silence after Satluj was taken down from ZEE5 in India until further notice. He shared a powerful scene from the film on Instagram with a defiant message.

All about Anshula Kapoor's wedding: Venue, date, timing and arrangements Influencer Anshula Kapoor is all set to marry longtime boyfriend Rohan Thakkar in an intimate Mumbai ceremony. Here's everything we know so far about the wedding date, venue and celebrations.

Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj taken down from ZEE5 India until further notice Actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj, originally titled Punjab 95, has been taken down from ZEE5 India until further notice. The streaming platform said it is exploring legal options to bring the film back and reaffirmed its support for the makers and their creative vision.

Harbhajan Singh calls Diljit's Satluj a must-watch: Truth cannot remain buried Former cricketer and MP Harbhajan Singh has praised Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj after watching the film. He appreciated the actor-singer and director Honey Trehan for telling Jaswant Singh Khalra's story.

Sohail Khan recalls father Salim's semi-coma scare: Doctor said you might lose him On Alliance, Sohail Khan recalled the time his father Salim Khan slipped into a semi-coma during hospitalisation. He said the episode deepened his fear of losing his parents and changed how he spends time with them.

Anshula Kapoor drops kalire on Orry, Janhvi, Khushi at chooda ceremony. See pics Anshula Kapoor shared heartwarming pictures from her chooda ceremony ahead of her wedding. The photos feature Arjun Kapoor, Boney Kapoor, Janhvi Kapoor, Khushi Kapoor, Shanaya Kapoor and Orry.

Watch: Aamir Khan kisses Gauri, dances with wife after she calls him her protector Videos from actor Aamir Khan and his wife Gauri Spratt's wedding show the couple exchanging vows, dancing and sharing an emotional kiss. One clip also captures Gauri calling Aamir her protector before they officially accepted each other as husband and wife.

GDN trailer: R Madhavan brings legendary inventor GD Naidu's story to life The makers of GDN have released the official trailer of the R Madhavan-starrer, offering a glimpse into the inspiring life and struggles of legendary Indian inventor G.D. Naidu. The biographical period drama is directed by Krishnakumar Ramakumar.

Lock Upp 2: Shilpa Shinde joins Farah Khan's show as first wildcard contestant Shilpa Shinde has entered Lock Upp Season 2 as its first wildcard contestant. Her entry comes shortly after she made headlines over her admission regarding the 2017 sexual harassment complaint against producer Sanjay Kohli.

Aamir greets media in 1st public appearance after wedding, wife Gauri Spratt skips Aamir Khan greeted the media after marrying Gauri Spratt at his home. The appearance followed the release of the first official wedding photograph from their private ceremony.

Alliance: Niti Taylor reveals ex-boyfriend burnt her with cigarette, slapped her On Alliance, Niti Taylor said an ex-boyfriend burnt her with a cigarette and slapped her. Her account drew focus to surviving abuse and the vulnerability she felt when she was younger.

Lock Upp 2: Madhuri Grover faces backlash over 'rich should have more kids' remark On Lock Upp 2, Madhuri Jain Grover spoke about wanting a third child and made a remark linking childbirth to wealth and poverty. The comment drew sharp criticism online, overshadowing the episode's eviction and Kangana Ranaut's guest appearance.

general news.headline: Heavy rain, landslides snap Mumbai-Pune connectivity; trains stop, expressway shut Live UpdatesIn the wake of the heavy rain, at least 16 trains between Mumbai and Pune were cancelled, while nine were diverted. The Mumbai-Pune Expressway and the old highway also remain shut until further notice.

War made us stronger: UK-based Iranian woman travels 4,000 km to mourn KhameneiAs an unending stream of people continues to pour into the Grand Mosalla, Tehran presents a striking picture: a capital in mourning, a nation still carrying the wounds of war, and a leadership that, at least for now, appears to have succeeded in rallying much of Iran behind it.

Bhutan journalist doubles down after India debunks report on E20 petrol offerThe exchange has evolved into a dispute over the record itself, with The Bhutanese newspaper citing official Bhutanese correspondence and India's Petroleum Ministry maintaining that no OMC ever offered E20 petrol.

Mumbai-Pune Expressway 'missing link' shut 2 months after launch due to rain damageHeavy rain triggered a landslide near Tunnel 2 on the Missing Link, forcing an early diversion on the Pune-to-Mumbai carriageway. The closure pushed vehicles through Lonavala, worsening congestion and delaying commuters.

How Vijay is redefining Opposition politics: Target BJP, but tango with CentreFor C Joseph Vijay, opposing the BJP seemingly does not mean opposing the Centre. Even as he has positioned the BJP as his principal ideological enemy, the Tamil Nadu Chief Minister is pursuing a pragmatic working relationship with the Modi government, separating political battles from administrative priorities.

Neymar retires from international football after Brazil's World Cup exitFIFA World Cup: Neymar has retired from international football after Brazil's shock Round of 16 exit to Norway at the World Cup. The Selecao legend announced his decision moments after the defeat, ending a glittering 16-year career as Brazil's record goalscorer.

Netanyahu's India rejoinder to JD Vance's 'US Israel's only ally' remarkBenjamin Netanyahu responded with a touch of sarcasm, saying Israel had "some other friends" besides the White House. "We have some other friends, like a small country called India. It has 1.4 billion people, and boy, do we have tremendous support there," he said.

No tension, everyone has downloaded: When Diljit predicted Satluj's OTT removalDays before Satluj was removed from ZEE5 in India, actor Diljit Dosanjh appeared to predict the film's fate on the streaming platform. During an Instagram Live session, he said he was not worried about the possibility and had "no tension" over it.

SP Mookerjee: A life devoted to India's unity and progressThe young Syama Prasad was born into circumstances that could easily have assured him a protected and comfortable life. His father, Sir Ashutosh Mookerjee, was among the foremost educationists and intellectuals of his age. Yet, while destiny placed before him a path of privilege, his conscience led him towards one of sacrifice and national service.

Monsoon shifts gears: India records 11% surplus rain in first week of JulyThe southwest monsoon has revived sharply, pushing India into a rainfall surplus in early July. The active spell is expected to bring heavy rain to several states while supporting kharif sowing and reservoir levels.

He's mentally unstable: Government sources after Khawaja Asif attacks PM Modi.Indian government sources strongly countered Khawaja Asif after he questioned the Seychelles honour conferred on PM Narendra Modi, adding that the Pakistan minister's alleged mental instability was a "known fact."

Live: Severe waterlogging leaves cars submerged in Mumbai's suburbsMonsoon Tracker Live Updates: Heavy monsoon showers swept across several parts of the country on Sunday (July 5, 2026), with Mumbai witnessing severe disruption, while weather authorities issued alerts for Kerala, Odisha and Jharkhand. Delhi also experienced widespread rainfall as monsoon activity gathered pace. Mumbai bore the brunt of the spell after intense overnight rain led to the suspension of runway operations at the Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport for about an hour. Official figures showed that several parts of the city received more than 200 mm of rainfall over the past 24 hours, while a few locations recorded nearly 300 mm. In eastern India, a well-marked low-pressure area over the Bay of Bengal strengthened into a depression, prompting Odisha to place all its districts on high alert. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the weather system is expected to cross the north Odisha coast between Chandbali and Digha within the next day, bringing heavy rainfall to Odisha and neighbouring West Bengal. Kerala also remained under the influence of active monsoon conditions, with widespread showers reported across most parts of the state. The IMD issued orange and yellow alerts for multiple districts. Meanwhile, Jharkhand has been placed under a yellow alert for thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds across all 24 districts from July 6 to July 9. Stay tuned with indiatoday.in for all live updates.

Odisha woman's Muqabla dance is so good, internet can't stop watchingOdisha-based Instagram user Moti Behera has drawn wide attention with her dance reel on the peppy song Muqabla. The clip is a must-watch.

Sam Altman compares his child speaking two words together to GPT 5.6 doing new mathOpenAI CEO Sam Altman says that his child putting two words together for the first time made him just as amazed as the time when GPT 5.6 "discovered" new math.

12-year-old girl found dead in Bengal, man lynched over rape-murder suspicionA man accused of raping and murdering a 12-year-old girl in South 24 Parganas died after he was allegedly lynched by the victim's family and local residents. The girl's body was recovered from a pond after locals caught one accused, sparking protests and a heavy police deployment.

Want to become a crorepati through SIP? Here's how much to invest and for how longBecoming a crorepati is no longer just a dream for the wealthy. With the right SIP strategy and enough time, you could get there too, but how much do you really need to invest each month?

Satluj removed from OTT: Internet rallies behind Diljit Dosanjh's filmAfter ZEE5 removed Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj from its platform in India two days after release, the move triggered online outrage, with many backing the film.

Mamata claims 'house arrest' over bid to meet Baruipur victim's familyIn a post on X, the party alleged that the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP)-led administration in West Bengal, after learning of Banerjee's proposed visit, deployed a large police force and erected barricades outside her residence to stop her from leaving for Baruipur.

In first E20 protest, motorists claim drop in mileage, choked car fuel systemFrom stalled engines and falling mileage to costly repairs, allegations made on social media were heard on the streets of Delhi as people protested against the implementation of the ethanol-blending policy. Motorists at Delhi's Jantar Mantar claimed E20 petrol was damaging their cars, and that they had evidence of that.

Great injustice reversed: Trump on FIFA's shocking reversal of US striker's banFIFA World Cup 2026: US President Donald Trump welcomed FIFA's decision to suspend the implementation of Folarin Balogun's match ban, clearing the USA striker to face Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16.

England survive the fires of Azteca to end Mexico's World Cup dreamEngland survived a dramatic Azteca thriller to beat co-hosts Mexico 3-2 and book their place in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals. Thomas Tuchel's side overcame a hostile atmosphere, controversy and relentless pressure to keep their title dream alive.

Rekha Gupta invokes Mamata's 'who is Amit Shah?' remark to target KejriwalArvind Kejriwal's 'Who are you?' reply to a BJP post on Nitin Nabin triggered a sharp response from Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta. The exchange adds to the BJP-AAP's increasingly public confrontation on social media ahead of key political contests.

How Mysore's Hyder Ali and Tipu Sultan helped America gain IndependenceAs the US marks 250 years since its Declaration of Independence, here's why Hyder Ali and Tipu Sultan, the rulers of India's Mysore, were admired in America during its war of independence against Britain. This is how the war in Mysore stretched British power, which played a decisive role in the American War of Independence.

Man finds rain-soaked diary on roadside, every page tells a heartwarming storyA man found a rain-soaked diary found on a roadside and appealed for help tracing its owner. The handwritten keepsake stirred viewers, who hoped the damaged memories could still be recovered.

sports.headline: I've lost my voice: Harry Kane revels after England oust Mexico in Azteca epic Harry Kane could barely speak after England's thrilling 3-2 win over Mexico in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16, admitting he had "lost his voice" after celebrating a famous victory with teammates and travelling supporters at the Azteca Stadium.

Watch: Ochoa retires in tears as England end Mexico's World Cup dream Guillermo Ochoa's storied career ended in heartbreak on Sunday as the legendary goalkeeper retired after Mexico's 3-2 defeat to England in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16.

England survive the fires of Azteca to end Mexico's World Cup dream England survived a dramatic Azteca thriller to beat co-hosts Mexico 3-2 and book their place in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals. Thomas Tuchel's side overcame a hostile atmosphere, controversy and relentless pressure to keep their title dream alive.

Why Bruno Guimaraes took Brazil's penalty over Vini Jr vs Norway? Ancelotti explains Carlo Ancelotti revealed Bruno Guimaraes was Brazil's third-choice penalty taker against Norway after Neymar and Raphinha were unavailable. The Newcastle midfielder's miss proved costly as Brazil crashed out of the FIFA World Cup.

Neymar retires from international football after Brazil's World Cup exit FIFA World Cup: Neymar has retired from international football after Brazil's shock Round of 16 exit to Norway at the World Cup. The Selecao legend announced his decision moments after the defeat, ending a glittering 16-year career as Brazil's record goalscorer.

MEX 2-3 ENG Highlights: England knock Mexico out of World Cup Mexico (Mexico) vs England (ENG) Highlights, FIFA World Cup Round of 16: England have booked their place in the World Cup quarter-finals with a hard-fought 3-2 victory over Mexico. After racing into a commanding lead, England survived relentless late pressure with a determined defensive display to end Mexico's World Cup campaign.

Erling Haaland ends Brazil's sleepy dream of a sixth World Cup with just 2 shots Carlo Ancelotti built a Brazil that refused to lose control. Against Norway, they controlled so little of the game that Erling Haaland only needed two chances to end their dreams of a sixth FIFA World Cup title.

Neymar sobs uncontrollably after Brazil's shock World Cup exit vs Norway: Watch Neymar was left inconsolable after Brazil's shock 2-1 defeat to Norway ended their FIFA World Cup campaign. The veteran forward broke down in tears following the final whistle, with footage capturing the heartbreak of another painful exit.

Jannik Sinner scripts another slice of history, sweeps into Wimbledon quarters Defending champion Jannik Sinner stormed into his fifth consecutive Wimbledon quarterfinal with a dominant straight-sets win over Shintaro Mochizuki, extending his winning streak at the All England Club to 11.

Cristiano Ronaldo confirms he's playing his last World Cup Cristiano Ronaldo confirmed the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be the last of his illustrious career, saying he wants to savour every moment of the tournament. The Portugal captain also expressed hope that his side's campaign against Spain will not be his final match.

Sabalenka vents frustration after Wimbledon exit: Want to get completely drunk Aryna Sabalenka admitted she wanted to get completely drunk after her Wimbledon title hopes ended with a straight-sets defeat to Naomi Osaka, who outplayed the World No. 1 to reach her maiden quarter-final at the All England Club.

BRA 1-2 NOR Highlights: Haaland leads Norway to knock Brazil out in R16Brazil (BRA) vs Norway (NOR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup: (BRA 1-2 NOR) Norway knock Brazil out of the 2026 FIFA World Cup with a famous victory. Erling Haaland's brace and Orjan Nyland's heroics seal a quarter-final spot, while Neymar's late penalty proves only a consolation for the Selecao.

Aryna Sabalenka crashes out as Naomi Osaka reaches maiden Wimbledon quartersNaomi Osaka defeated World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka 6-2, 7-6(2) in a statement Centre Court display on Sunday to reach her maiden Wimbledon quarter-final.

Ruthless Australia blow away England, clinch 7th Women's T20 World Cup titleAustralia produced a ruthless, dominant performance to crush England and win a record-extending seventh Women's T20 World Cup title with a seven-wicket victory at Lord's Cricket Ground.

Great injustice reversed: Trump on FIFA's shocking reversal of US striker's banFIFA World Cup 2026: US President Donald Trump welcomed FIFA's decision to suspend the implementation of Folarin Balogun's match ban, clearing the USA striker to face Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16.

Novak Djokovic breaks Roger Federer's Wimbledon record, reaches quarter-finalsNovak Djokovic battled past Roman Safiullin to reach the Wimbledon 2026 quarter-finals, registering a record 106th men's singles win at SW19 to surpass Roger Federer. Jessica Pegula and Karolina Muchova also advanced to the last eight.

Watch: Nat Sciver-Brunt, baby Theo create unforgettable T20 World Cup final momentEngland captain Nat Sciver-Brunt melted hearts by walking onto the Lord's field with her infant son Theo before the Women's T20 World Cup final, showing the growing acceptance of motherhood in elite cricket.

Lewis Hamilton takes British GP podium in P3 after late drama, Leclerc winsFormula 1: Lewis Hamilton finished third for Ferrari at the British Grand Prix in a race defined by late safety car drama and strategic calls that reshaped the podium. Charles Leclerc won the race, while George Russell took second as late incidents, including Max Verstappen's crash, neutralised the final stages.

Why Cabo Verde's 51st Independence day felt extra special this yearFIFA World Cup 2026: Cabo Verde returned home to packed streets and a thunderous airport welcome after their World Cup exit. The reception on the nation's 51st Independence Day showed how this run had become a marker of national pride.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for 2027 World Cup? Ex-spinner makes bold ODI predictionL Sivaramakrishnan has urged India to consider Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for the 2027 World Cup. The former spinner said the teenager should be backed in white-ball cricket as a long-term match-winner.

Day 7 Highlights: Sinner beats Mochizuki, advances to quarterfinalsWimbledon Day 7 Live Score: Jannik Sinner beat Japan's Shintaro Mochizuki and advanced to the quarterfinals. Earlier, Novak Djokovic beat Roman Safiullin on Centre Court and advanced to the quarters. Aryna Sabalenka has been knocked out after losing to Naomi Osaka.

Not Cape Verde hero, Real Madrid sign Dutch star in right-back positionReal Madrid have completed the signing of Netherlands international Denzel Dumfries from Inter Milan, ending speculation over their right-back search. This brings a momentary end to Sidny Lopez Cabral dream of playing under Jose Mourinho for the second time in his career.

No pills, no peace? England deny Viagra claims as noisy Mexican fans ruin sleepFIFA World Cup Round of 16: Thomas Tuchel denied reports that England used Viagra before facing Mexico at the Azteca. The odd exchange came as noisy Mexican fans outside the team hotel added to England's challenge of playing at a higher altitude.

Gurnoor impresses with 10-wicket haul, Sudharsan with twin hundreds vs SL AFast bowler Gurnoor Brar ripped through Sri Lanka A in the second and final unofficial Test of the tour. India No.3, Sai Sudharsan, playing as an opener in the series, scored twin centuries as well.

Sanju Samson dropped, are you kidding me? Manjrekar stirs the pot after T20I snubSanjay Manjrekar questioned India's call to drop Sanju Samson for the second T20I against England despite handing Vaibhav Sooryavanshi a record debut. The decision and India's defeat sharpened scrutiny on team balance, considering the abundance of left-handers in the batting unit.

FIFA World Cup: Brazil, England sweat over injuries ahead of Round of 16 gamesBrazil and England are sweating over key injury concerns ahead of their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clashes. Both Carlo Ancelotti and Thomas Tuchel are readying for tough challenges.

Ghost banished: Grigor Dimitrov rewrites Wimbledon script to beat BerrettiniGrigor Dimitrov beat Matteo Berrettini in five sets to reach the Wimbledon fourth round. The win completed his comeback from last year's tearful injury exit and revived his hopes of reaching a Slam quarterfinal for the first time in two years.

Brazil play Norway in Round of 16. Will strangest World Cup curse strike again?Brazil are playing Norway in their Round of 16 clash in the FIFA World Cup. The 5-time champions will face a steep test against Erling Haaland and Martin Odegaard's side.

Shan Masood sacked, Babar Azam back: Pakistan's cricket musical chair on againBabar Azam returns as Pakistan's Test captain for the upcoming tours of the West Indies and England, despite a prolonged lean patch. He replaces the sacked Shan Masood, who paid the price for a dismal run of 12 defeats in 16 Tests.

India's mini-slump in T20Is: What's gone wrong with Shreyas Iyer's team?India slumped to their third loss in four matches under the captaincy of Shreyas Iyer. After their defeat in Manchester, Ishan Kishan said that it was important for the team to band together right now.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
